hero.title: Palo Alto Security Platform Modernisation
hero.subtitle: NGFW • Prisma Access • Cortex XDR/XSIAM • Strata Cloud Manager • Identity-aligned SASE
hero.description: I modernise Palo Alto Networks estates for enterprises that need architecture-first clarity, reduced operational noise, and commercially optimised renewals. From assessment through programme execution to ongoing oversight, I deliver measurable improvements you can defend at the board.
hero.footer: Platform-first security architecture • Regulated sector experience • Board-ready output

about.title: Who is
paragraphs: I operate as a senior delivery authority and platform strategist, turning fragmented Palo Alto deployments into cohesive, high-performing architectures. I focus on NGFW, Prisma Access/SASE, Cortex, XSOAR/XSIAM, and Strata Cloud Manager—aligning them to identity, governance, and commercial realities.

Engagements are structured, direct, and outcome-driven: rapid assessments, executable roadmaps, and modernisation programmes governed by clear exit criteria. No resale, no junior churn—just platform-first delivery that reduces risk and operating noise while protecting budget.

highlights.title: Platform-first Architecture
highlights.description: Unifies NGFW, Prisma Access, Cortex, XSOAR/XSIAM, and identity into a coherent operating model

highlights.title: Executive-ready Outcomes
highlights.description: Board-ready findings, defensible roadmaps, and commercial optimisation tied to renewals

highlights.title: Regulated Experience
highlights.description: Finance, energy, public sector, and legal—identity-aligned controls and audit support

projects.title: Platform Modernisation Assessment (Global Bank)
projects.description: Three-week RAG assessment across NGFW, Prisma Access, Cortex, and XSIAM with identity alignment and renewal prep.
highlights: Tool overlap removal saved 18% on renewals

projects.title: SASE & Cortex Programme (Energy)
projects.description: NGFW to SASE uplift with Prisma Access optimisation, Cortex tuning, and SOC integration for critical sites.
highlights: Identity-aware policies rolled out to 14 regions
Milestone-based governance with clear exit criteria

projects.title: XSOAR / XSIAM Onboarding (Public Sector)
projects.description: Deployed XSOAR playbooks and aligned XSIAM telemetry for regulated SOC with audit-ready evidence.

projects.title: Retained Product Oversight (Legal)
projects.description: Quarterly posture reviews, roadmap alignment, and renewal guidance across Palo Alto portfolio.
highlights: Executive cadence without running the SOC

achievements: Led platform modernisation programmes across NGFW, Prisma Access, Cortex, and XSIAM
Delivered RAG assessments with board-ready outputs and 90–180 day roadmaps
Reduced SOC noise and clarified tool ownership with identity-aligned controls
Optimised licensing ahead of renewals to reduce cost without sacrificing coverage
Architected SASE uplifts and Cortex rollouts for multi-region deployments
Rationalised SIEM/SOC integrations to cut alert noise and improve MTTR
Introduced operating models with clear ownership, KPIs, and exit criteria
Guided regulated sector bids with platform-first reference architectures
Implemented NGFW and remote access services with strong identity integration
Built change and release discipline to protect uptime during rollouts
Partnered with SOC teams on tuning and runbook definition

items.description: Delivered network and security operations across mixed-vendor estates, focusing on stability and policy hygiene.
Supported enterprise change, incident response, and configuration management for mission-critical systems.

items.name: Palo Alto Networks Certified Network Security Engineer (PCNSE)

items.text: Clear, platform-first thinking that gave us an executable roadmap and immediate savings ahead of renewal.
items.fullText: Clear, platform-first thinking that gave us an executable roadmap and immediate savings ahead of renewal. The board appreciated the simplicity of the findings and the confidence to make decisions without delaying our renewal window.
items.author: Sarah L.

items.text: Reduced SOC noise, aligned identity, and got Prisma delivering. Senior-only team—no churn.
items.fullText: Reduced SOC noise, aligned identity, and got Prisma delivering. Senior-only team—no churn. We finally have a unified operating model across Palo Alto instead of tool silos.
items.author: David M.

items.text: Board-ready assessment in three weeks with defensible cost optimisation. Exactly what we needed.
items.fullText: Board-ready assessment in three weeks with defensible cost optimisation. Exactly what we needed to take to Audit & Risk before committing to renewals and expansion.
items.author: Helen R.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.content: This Privacy Policy explains how I collect, use, and protect your personal information when you visit this website and submit contact forms. I am committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring transparency about how your data is handled.

sections.title: 2. Information I Collect
sections.content: When you submit a contact form on this website, I collect the following information: your name, email address, company name (optional), service interest, and message content. This information is collected solely for the purpose of responding to your inquiry.

sections.title: 3. How I Use Your Information
sections.content: Your information is used only to: respond to your contact inquiry, provide relevant services or information, and improve my services based on feedback. I will not use your information for marketing purposes without your explicit consent.

sections.title: 4. Data Storage & Security
sections.content: Your submitted information is stored securely in a managed database. I use industry-standard security measures to protect your data from unauthorized access, alteration, or deletion. However, no online transmission is completely secure, and I cannot guarantee absolute security.

sections.title: 5. Data Retention
sections.content: Contact form submissions are retained for as long as necessary to respond to your inquiry and maintain business records. You can request deletion of your personal information at any time by contacting me directly.

sections.title: 6. Third-Party Services
sections.content: This website uses GitHub API to display public GitHub profile and repositories. GitHub is a third party, and their privacy policy applies to any data collected through their services. I do not share your contact form data with third parties.

sections.title: 7. Cookies
sections.content: This website may use cookies to enhance your browsing experience. These are used only for technical functionality and preference storage. You can disable cookies in your browser settings if you prefer.

sections.title: 8. Your Rights
sections.content: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal information. If you wish to exercise these rights, please contact me directly via the contact form or email.

sections.title: 9. Changes to This Policy
sections.content: I may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. Your continued use of this website constitutes your acceptance of the updated Privacy Policy.

sections.title: 10. Contact
sections.content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or your personal information, please contact me through the contact form on this website.
